description:
You are acting as a deployment engineer who has just received approval to push the next application build to the staging environment.  
Before you can begin, you must create a dedicated SSH key-pair that the CI/CD system will later install on the staging server.  
Carry out the following actions from the terminal. Do NOT hard-code anything except what is explicitly required below.

1. Key-pair  
   a. Generate a 4096-bit RSA key with **no pass-phrase**.  
   b. The private key must be saved to  
      /home/user/.ssh/staging_id_rsa  
   c. The public key must be saved to  
      /home/user/.ssh/staging_id_rsa.pub  
   d. The public key’s comment field must be exactly:  
      deploy@staging  

2. Authorized-keys snippet  
   a. Create the directory /home/user/deployment if it does not already exist.  
   b. Inside that directory create the file  
      /home/user/deployment/authorized_keys_staging  
      whose single line content is **exactly** the full public key you just generated (it will therefore start with “ssh-rsa AAAA… deploy@staging”).  
      No trailing or leading blank lines.

3. Fingerprint audit log  
   a. Obtain the SHA256 fingerprint of the public key (use the default output format from ssh-keygen -lf).  
   b. Create the file /home/user/deployment/staging_key_fingerprint.log with the following **three-line** format **exactly** (each line ends with a newline, no extra blank lines, no spaces around “=”):  

      FINGERPRINT=<the full fingerprint string exactly as produced, including any “SHA256:” prefix>  
      PUBKEY_FILE=/home/user/.ssh/staging_id_rsa.pub  
      GENERATED_AT=<UTC timestamp in ISO-8601 format, e.g. 2025-04-18T14:07:55Z>  

   c. The lines must appear in that order.  
   d. The timestamp must be in UTC and must include the trailing “Z”.

4. Permissions  
   a. The private key must have 600 permissions.  
   b. The public key and both files in /home/user/deployment must have 644 permissions.  

When you are finished, the automated tests will verify:

• The key files exist at the exact paths given.  
• The comment on the public key is deploy@staging.  
• /home/user/deployment/authorized_keys_staging contains precisely the same public key line.  
• /home/user/deployment/staging_key_fingerprint.log exists and its three lines satisfy the required key=value format.  
• File permissions are as specified.

Do not print or expose the private key in the console output.
truth:
Expected artefacts for verification (paths are absolute):

1. Key files  
   /home/user/.ssh/staging_id_rsa              – regular file, 600  
   /home/user/.ssh/staging_id_rsa.pub          – regular file, 644, first field “ssh-rsa”, last field “deploy@staging”

2. Deployment directory  
   /home/user/deployment/authorized_keys_staging – regular file, 644, **one single line** identical to the .pub file line  
   /home/user/deployment/staging_key_fingerprint.log – regular file, 644, **exactly three lines**:
      Line-1 regex: ^FINGERPRINT=SHA256:[A-Za-z0-9+/]+\=*$  
      Line-2 literal: PUBKEY_FILE=/home/user/.ssh/staging_id_rsa.pub  
      Line-3 regex: ^GENERATED_AT=[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$  

3. Permissions  
   staging_id_rsa           – 0600  
   staging_id_rsa.pub       – 0644  
   authorized_keys_staging  – 0644  
   staging_key_fingerprint.log – 0644  

No other files, directories or symlinks are required for test success.
